title: Preliminary Report on Foreign Holdings of U.S. Securities at End-June 2025
summary: The Treasury released preliminary data from its annual survey of foreign portfolio holdings of U.S. securities as of June 30, 2025, showing total foreign holdings of $35,334 billion, up 14.4% from $30,881 billion in June 2024. The survey was conducted jointly by Treasury, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, and the Federal Reserve Board, with final results expected on April 30, 2026.
key_points: Total foreign holdings of U.S. securities measured at $35,334 billion as of June 30, 2025, compared to $30,881 billion in June 2024 (+$4,453 billion increase)
U.S. equities holdings: $19,843 billion (up from $16,878 billion); U.S. long-term debt: $13,839 billion (up from $12,688 billion)
U.S. short-term debt securities holdings: $1,652 billion (up from $1,314 billion)
Foreign official holdings reported at $6,904 billion (up from $6,598 billion)
Asset-backed securities (ABS) within long-term debt: $1,626 billion; non-ABS securities: $12,213 billion
Top investing countries: United Kingdom ($3,461B), Cayman Islands ($3,177B), Canada ($2,898B), Japan ($2,883B), Luxembourg ($2,640B)
Survey conducted jointly by Treasury, Federal Reserve Bank of New York, and Federal Reserve Board; final results due April 30, 2026
why_it_matters: This TIC (Treasury International Capital) survey data provides comprehensive information on foreign ownership of U.S. securities, which is critical for understanding U.S. external financing needs, dollar demand dynamics, and potential sensitivities to foreign selling. The 14.4% year-over-year increase reflects growing foreign investment in U.S. assets during the period. Foreign official holdings data is particularly relevant for assessing central bank demand for Treasuries.
macro_context: The survey captures significant cross-border capital flows into U.S. securities, with foreign holdings now representing a substantial portion of U.S. market capitalization and Treasury outstanding. The composition shift toward equities (+$2.965T) exceeded debt growth (+$1.489T combined), suggesting increased foreign appetite for U.S. corporate equity exposure during the measurement period.
important_numbers: $13,839 billion (U.S. long-term debt held by foreigners)
$1,652 billion (U.S. short-term debt held by foreigners)
notes: This is preliminary data; final results with additional detail and possible revisions will be released April 30, 2026. The source is highly authoritative (joint Treasury/Fed release) and directly relevant to macro catalysts through its implications for external financing and capital flow dynamics. Data is 8 months old relative to today (2026-04-10), consistent with the standard TIC survey reporting lag.
